Name: UPhysicalMaterial
Description: Physical materials are used to define the response of a physical object when interacting dynamically with the world.

Variables.Name: Friction
Variables.Description: Friction value of surface, controls how easily things can slide on this surface (0 is frictionless, higher values increase the amount of friction)

Variables.Name: FrictionCombineMode
Variables.Description: Friction combine mode, controls how friction is computed for multiple materials.

Variables.Name: bOverrideFrictionCombineMode
Variables.Description: If set we will use the FrictionCombineMode of this material, instead of the FrictionCombineMode found in the project settings.

Variables.Name: Restitution
Variables.Description: Restitution or 'bounciness' of this surface, between 0 (no bounce) and 1 (outgoing velocity is same as incoming).

Variables.Name: RestitutionCombineMode
Variables.Description: Restitution combine mode, controls how restitution is computed for multiple materials.

Variables.Name: bOverrideRestitutionCombineMode
Variables.Description: If set we will use the RestitutionCombineMode of this material, instead of the RestitutionCombineMode found in the project settings.

Variables.Name: Density
Variables.Description: Used with the shape of the object to calculate its mass properties. The higher the number, the heavier the object. g per cubic cm.

Variables.Name: RaiseMassToPower
Variables.Description:
Used to adjust the way that mass increases as objects get larger. This is applied to the mass as calculated based on a 'solid' object. 
	 	In actuality, larger objects do not tend to be solid, and become more like 'shells' (e.g. a car is not a solid piece of metal).
	 	Values are clamped to 1 or less.

Variables.Name: DestructibleDamageThresholdScale
Variables.Description: How much to scale the damage threshold by on any destructible we are applied to

Variables.Name: SurfaceType
Variables.Description: To edit surface type for your project, use ProjectSettingsPhysicsPhysicalSurface section

Variables.Name: TireFrictionScale
Variables.Description: DEPRECATED - Overall tire friction scalar for every type of tire. This value is multiplied against our parents' values.

Variables.Name: TireFrictionScales
Variables.Description: DEPRECATED - Tire friction scales for specific types of tires. These values are multiplied against our parents' values.
